title: Kth Element of Two Sorted Arrays

content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two sorted arrays **AAA** and **BBB** of sizes **MMM** and **NNN**. Chef wants to know the **KKKth element** in the array obtained after merging **AAA** and **BBB** into a single sorted array.

Chef does **not** want to build the merged array explicitly. He only needs the value of the **KKKth element** in the merged sorted order.

Your task is to help Chef find this element.

Function Declaration

**Function Name:**



**findKthElementfindKthElementfindKthElement**



**Parameters:**



**aaa**
A sorted array of size **MMM** in non-decreasing order.
**bbb**
A sorted array of size **NNN** in non-decreasing order.
**kkk**
The 1-based position of the element to be found in the merged sorted order.



**Return Value:**



Returns a number representing the **KKKth element** in the merged sorted array.


Constraints
1≤M,N≤1041 \le M, N \le 10^41≤M,N≤104
0≤a[i],b[i]≤1090 \le a[i], b[i] \le 10^90≤a[i],b[i]≤109
1≤K≤M+N1 \le K \le M + N1≤K≤M+N
Both arrays are sorted in non-decreasing order.

Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers **MMM** and **NNN**.
The second line contains **MMM** space-separated integers representing array **AAA**.
The third line contains **NNN** space-separated integers representing array **BBB**.
The fourth line contains an integer **KKK**.



Output Format

Print a single integer — the **Kth element** of the merged sorted array.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Merged array = [2, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, 25]. The 4th element is 10.

Explanation:
Merged array = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]. The 5th element is 5.
